req.title: Open savings account
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three critical test dimensions: (1) a valid verified customer depositing above the minimum threshold should receive a 201 with an assigned interest tier — covered by EP positive case; (2) a deposit below $100 must return 422 — covered by NEG negative case; (3) the exact boundary of $100 must succeed — covered by BVA boundary case. An additional DT case covers the unverified customer path, and an STC case validates the state transition from 'no account' to 'active account with tier'. All tests use a shared fixture for the verified-customer auth token and a cleanup hook to ensure idempotency.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit
preconditions: API base URL configured in environment
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer_token>
expected: Response body contains 'interest_tier' field with a non-null value

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_returns_422
preconditions: API base URL configured in environment
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer_token>
expected: Response body contains an 'error' or 'detail' field describing the minimum balance violation
No account is created in the system

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_exactly_minimum_boundary
preconditions: API base URL configured in environment
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer_token>
expected: Response body contains 'interest_tier' with a non-null value

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_unverified_customer_rejected
preconditions: API base URL configured in environment
actions: Obtain auth token for an UNVERIFIED customer via <fixture: unverified_customer_token>
expected: Response body contains an error indicating the customer is not verified
trace: R-FIN-002: precondition customer.verified=true — decision table row where verified=false
R-FIN-002: account must NOT be created for unverified customer

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_state_transition_to_active
preconditions: customer has NO existing savings account (state: no-account)
API base URL configured in environment
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer_token>
Confirm no savings account exists for customer via GET /accounts/savings
expected: Pre-state: no savings account exists (404 or empty list)
GET on new account returns status 'active'
Account transitions from non-existent to active state with tier assigned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import os

BASE_URL = os.environ.get("BANKING_API_URL", "http://localhost:8080")

@pytest.fixture
def verified_customer_token():
    return <fixture: verified_customer_token>

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup_account(verified_customer_token):
    created_ids = []
    yield created_ids
    for acct_id in created_ids:
        <helper_fn: delete_account>(acct_id, verified_customer_token)

def test_open_savings_account_state_transition_to_active(verified_customer_token, cleanup_account):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}"}
    pre = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/savings", headers=headers)
    assert pre.status_code in (404, 200)
    if pre.status_code == 200:
        assert pre.json().get("data", []) == []
    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/savings", json={"initial_deposit": 250.00}, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    acct_id = body["account_id"]
    cleanup_account.append(acct_id)
    detail = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/accounts/savings/{acct_id}", headers=headers)
    assert detail.status_code == 200
    assert detail.json().get("status") == "active"
    assert detail.json().get("interest_tier") is not None
